---
title: "UG_BearBlood_RNASeq_analysis_09.30.2025"
date: "2025-09-30"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(tibble)
getwd()
```

# Load Data
```{r}
# Load Raw Counts matrix from FeatureCounts
featureCounts_cts <- read.delim("brownBear_Blood_RNAseq_rawCounts_q0_q24_sd_413_09.26.2025.txt", header = TRUE, comment.char = "#")
gene_lengths <- featureCounts_cts %>% dplyr::select(Geneid, Length)

# Load metadata
metadata <- read_tsv("bearBlood_RNASeq_UG_metadata_v2.tsv", col_names = T)
metadata$season <- str_split_fixed(metadata$sample_name,"_",3)[,3]
season_order <- c(
  "Active" = 1, # June (6) -- 1 -- day 1 -- 0
  "Fall" = 120, # October(10) -- 4 -- day 120 --3.9
  "Hibernation" = 189, # January(1) -- 7 -- day 189 --6.2
  "Spring" = 259, # March(3) -- 9 -- day 259--8.5
  "SpringT2" = 273 # March 2 weeks later(4) -- 10 -- day 273  --8.96
)
metadata <- metadata %>% mutate(time = season_order[season],bear = str_split_fixed(sample_name, "_", 3)[, 2])

metadata_nondepleted <- metadata %>%
  filter(str_split_fixed(sample_name, "_", 3)[, 1] == "Nondepleted")
write_csv(metadata_nondepleted, "nondepleted_metadata.csv")

# Load Raw Counts Matrix with fixed sample names
cts_fixed <- read.delim("brownBear_Blood_RNAseq_rawCounts_sd413_cleanHeaders_finalMatrix.txt", header = TRUE, comment.char = "#")

metadata_nondepleted

sum(metadata_nondepleted$season == "Spring")
```
## Dotplot and T-test of rRNA and hemogolbin
```{r}
cts <- cts_fixed

rownames(cts) <- cts$Geneid
cts <- cts %>% dplyr::select(-Geneid)

rRNA_28S <- read.delim("ursus_arctos_28S_rRNA.txt", header = TRUE, comment.char = "#")
rRNA_18S <- read.delim("ursus_arctos_18S_rRNA.txt", header = TRUE, comment.char = "#")

rRNA_genes <- c(rRNA_28S$Symbol, rRNA_18S$Symbol)

hemoglobin_genes <- c("LOC113248288",   # Hb beta
                      "LOC113248289",   # Hb beta
                      "LOC113245361",   # Hb alpha
                      "LOC125283438",   # Hb alpha
                      "HBM")            # Hb mu

# Hemoglobin counts per sample
hb_counts <- colSums(cts[rownames(cts) %in% hemoglobin_genes, ])
total_counts <- colSums(cts)

# Fraction (%)
hb_fraction <- hb_counts / total_counts * 100

df_hb2 <- data.frame(
  sample = names(hb_fraction),
  percent = hb_fraction
)

# Label condition
df_hb2 <- df_hb2 %>%
  mutate(
    condition = if_else(grepl("^Depleted", sample), "Depleted", "Non-Depleted"),
    rna = "Globin",
    name = str_split_fixed(sample, "_", 4)[, 2],
    season = str_split_fixed(sample, "_", 4)[, 3],
    lab = paste0(name,"_",season)
  )

# rRNA counts per sample
rrna_counts <- colSums(cts[rownames(cts) %in% rRNA_genes, ])
total_counts <- colSums(cts)

# Fraction (%)
rna_fraction <- rrna_counts / total_counts * 100

df_rna2 <- data.frame(
  sample = names(rna_fraction),
  percent = rna_fraction
)

# Label condition
df_rna2 <- df_rna2 %>%
  mutate(
    condition = if_else(grepl("^Depleted", sample), "Depleted", "Non-Depleted"),
    rna = "rRNA",
    name = str_split_fixed(sample, "_", 4)[, 2],
    season = str_split_fixed(sample, "_", 4)[, 3],
    lab = paste0(name,"_",season)
  )

df_plot <- rbind(df_hb2, df_rna2)



ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = condition, y = percent, fill = condition)) +
  #geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.7, size = 2, aes(color = condition)) +
  facet_wrap(~rna) +
  #geom_text(aes(label = lab), size = 3, check_overlap = TRUE) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = NULL,
    x = "Condition",
    y = "Percent of transcripts (%) log-scale"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Depleted" = "orange", "Non-Depleted" = "steelblue"),
    labels = c("Depleted" = "rRNA/Globin Depleted",
               "Non-Depleted" = "poly(A)-selected")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Depleted" = "orange", "Non-Depleted" = "steelblue"),
    labels = c("Depleted" = "rRNA/Globin Depleted",
               "Non-Depleted" = "poly(A)-selected")
  ) + 
  labs(fill = "Library Type", color = "Library Type") +
  scale_x_discrete(
      labels = c(
        "Depleted" = "rRNA/Globin Depleted",
        "Non-Depleted" = "poly(A)-selected"
      )
    ) + 
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)
  )
ggsave("depletion_vs_polyAselection.pdf", dpi=600,h=6,w=7)



```


# Data cleanup
```{r}
# Load rRNA genes and remove from counts matrix
rRNA_28S <- read.delim("ursus_arctos_28S_rRNA.txt", header = TRUE, comment.char = "#")
rRNA_18S <- read.delim("ursus_arctos_18S_rRNA.txt", header = TRUE, comment.char = "#")
hemoglobin_genes <- c("LOC113248288",   # Hb beta
                      "LOC113248289",   # Hb beta
                      "LOC113245361",   # Hb alpha
                      "LOC125283438"   # Hb alpha
                      )           

abundant_genes <- c(rRNA_28S$Symbol, rRNA_18S$Symbol)
cts_fixed <- cts_fixed[(!cts_fixed$Geneid %in% abundant_genes), ] # there's 116 18S/28S rRNA genes in the counts matrix

# Get a matrix of the gene names and their lengths for TPM
cts_gene_lengths <- featureCounts_cts %>% dplyr::select(Geneid, Length)

# Load y chromosome genes and remove from matrix
y_chr <- read.delim("ursus_arctos_y_chr_genes.tsv")
cts_fixed <- cts_fixed[!cts_fixed$Geneid %in% y_chr$Symbol, ] # There's 74 y-chromosome genes found in the counts matrix

write_tsv(cts_fixed, "brownBear_Blood_RNAseq_rawCounts_cleanedUp_10.20.2025.tsv")

x_chr <- read.delim("ursus_arctos_x_chr_genes.tsv")
cts_x_chr <- cts_fixed[cts_fixed$Geneid %in% x_chr$Symbol, ]

# Finalize counts marix for DESeq2
rownames(cts_fixed) <- cts_fixed$Geneid
cts_fixed <-  cts_fixed %>% dplyr::select(-Geneid)

```

## Correlation
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)

# ------------ TPM ------------------------------------------------------------
gene_length_corr <- gene_lengths %>% 
  filter(Geneid %in% rownames(cts_fixed))

x <- cts_fixed / gene_length_corr$Length

# Calculate column sums for normalization
col_sums <- colSums(x, na.rm = TRUE)

# Normalize to TPM scale
tpm.mat <- t(t(x) * 1e6 / col_sums)

# Convert to data frame
tpm.df <- as.data.frame(tpm.mat)

# Split depleted vs nondepleted
tpm.depleted <- tpm.df %>% 
  dplyr::select(starts_with("Depleted_"))

new_names_dep <- paste0(
  str_split_fixed(colnames(tpm.depleted), "_", 4)[,2], "_",
  str_split_fixed(colnames(tpm.depleted), "_", 4)[,3]
)
colnames(tpm.depleted) <- new_names_dep

tpm.nondepleted <- tpm.df %>% 
  dplyr::select(starts_with("Nondepleted_"))

new_names_nondep <- paste0(
  str_split_fixed(colnames(tpm.nondepleted), "_", 4)[,2], "_",
  str_split_fixed(colnames(tpm.nondepleted), "_", 4)[,3]
)
colnames(tpm.nondepleted) <- new_names_nondep

# Add gene IDs
tpm.depleted$gene    <- rownames(tpm.depleted)
tpm.nondepleted$gene <- rownames(tpm.nondepleted)

# ------------ median log2(TPM+1) per season ---------------------------------

dep_long <- tpm.depleted %>%
  pivot_longer(-gene, names_to = "sample", values_to = "dep") %>%
  mutate(season = str_split_fixed(sample, "_", 2)[,2])

nondep_long <- tpm.nondepleted %>%
  pivot_longer(-gene, names_to = "sample", values_to = "nondep") %>%
  mutate(season = str_split_fixed(sample, "_", 2)[,2])

dep_season_median <- dep_long %>%
  group_by(gene, season) %>%
  summarise(med_tpm = median(dep, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(dep = log2(med_tpm + 1)) %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, season, dep)

nondep_season_median <- nondep_long %>%
  group_by(gene, season) %>%
  summarise(med_tpm = median(nondep, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(nondep = log2(med_tpm + 1)) %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, season, nondep)

# Merge depleted and nondepleted
df_all <- left_join(
  dep_season_median,
  nondep_season_median,
  by = c("gene", "season")
)

# ------------ fix season labels BEFORE correlations/plotting -----------------

df_all <- df_all %>%
  mutate(
    season = recode(
      season,
      "Spring" = "Spring 1",
      "SpringT2" = "Spring 2"
    ),
    season = factor(
      season,
      levels = c("Active", "Fall", "Hibernation", "Spring 1", "Spring 2")
    )
  )

# ------------ Spearman correlations per season -------------------------------

cors_stats <- df_all %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(
    rho = suppressWarnings(cor(nondep, dep, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")),
    p = suppressWarnings(cor.test(nondep, dep, method = "spearman")$p.value),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# facet-specific annotation positions
cor_spears_df <- cors_stats %>%
  left_join(
    df_all %>%
      group_by(season) %>%
      summarise(
        x = min(nondep, na.rm = TRUE),
        y = max(dep, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ),
    by = "season"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    p_fmt = case_when(
      is.na(p) ~ "NA",
      p < 1e-3 ~ "< 0.001",
      TRUE ~ formatC(p, format = "f", digits = 3)
    ),
    label = paste0("rho = ", sprintf("%.3f", rho), "\n", "p ", p_fmt)
  )

# ------------ plot -----------------------------------------------------------

p <- ggplot(df_all, aes(x = nondep, y = dep)) +
  geom_point(
    size = 0.7,
    alpha = 0.25,
    stroke = 0,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_abline(
    slope = 1,
    intercept = 0,
    linewidth = 0.5,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "firebrick"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = cor_spears_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1.1,
    size = 3.2,
    family = "sans"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~season, scales = "fixed", ncol = 5, drop = TRUE) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    x = "log2(median TPM + 1), Poly(A)-selected",
    y = "log2(median TPM + 1)\n(rRNA/hemoglobin-depleted)",
    title = "Expression concordance across seasons"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = "black", linewidth = 0.4),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 9, color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.3)
  )

p

ggsave("Supp_Figure3_final.pdf", plot = p, width = 10, height = 3.2, units = "in")
```

## Seq depth
```{r}
genes_detected <- colSums(tpm.mat > 1)  # or > 0.1
df_detect <- data.frame(
  sample = colnames(tpm.mat),
  genes_detected = genes_detected
) %>%
  mutate(condition = if_else(grepl("^Depleted", sample), "Depleted", "Nondepleted"))

ggplot(df_detect, aes(x = condition, y = genes_detected)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Library type",
    y = "Genes detected (TPM > 1)"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
    "Depleted" = "rRNA/Globin depleted",
    "Nondepleted" = "Poly(A) selected"
  )) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)
  )
```



## DESEQ2 Function
```{r}
run_DESeq2 <- function(cts,metadata) {
  library(DESeq2)
  library(IHW)
  
  # Extract sample names (columns of count matrix)
  samples <- colnames(cts)
  
  # Split sample names by "_"
  # Example: "Depleted_Adak_Active_male"
  bear <-  factor(str_split_fixed(samples, "_", 4)[,2])
  season <-  factor(str_split_fixed(samples, "_", 4)[,3])
  sex <-  factor(str_split_fixed(samples, "_", 4)[,4])
  age <- factor(metadata$birth)
  time <- factor(metadata$time)
  
  # Build DESeq2 metadata (colData)
  coldata <- DataFrame(season=season, bear=bear,sex=sex,age=age, time=time)
  rownames(coldata) <- colnames(cts)  
  print(coldata)
  
  # Construct DESeq2 dataset object
  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = cts,
    colData = coldata,
    design = ~ sex+season  # model both bear and season, the more important condition should be last
  )
  
  # Pre-filtering: remove genes with very low counts
  keep <- rowSums(counts(dds) >= 10) >= 2 # keep genes with at least 10 reads in ≥2 samples
  dds <- dds[keep, ]
  
  print(sum(keep)) 
    
  # Run DESeq2 normalization and DE analysis
  dds <- DESeq(dds)
  
  return(dds)
}

# Split counts by depletion status
cts.depleted <- cts_fixed %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("Depleted_"))
cts.nondepleted <- cts_fixed %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("Nondepleted_"))


# Run DESeq2 for each set
dds.nondepleted <- run_DESeq2(cts.nondepleted,metadata_nondepleted)

```

# PCA

function
```{r}
plot_PCA <- function(dds, gene_set){
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggrepel)
  
  # Perform variance-stabilizing transformation (VST) on count data
  # blind=TRUE should be used for comparing samples in a manner unbiased by prior information on samples
  vsd <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds, blind = TRUE)
  mat <- assay(vsd)
  
  # Remove bear-specific effects using limma's removeBatchEffect()
  # Adjust for differences due to individual bears while preserving seasonal effects
  mm <- model.matrix(~season, colData(vsd))
  mat <- limma::removeBatchEffect(mat, batch = vsd$bear, design = mm)
  assay(vsd) <- mat
  
  # Get PCA results including metadata
  pca <- plotPCA(vsd, intgroup = c("season", "bear","sex"), returnData = TRUE, ntop = 2000)
  percentVar <- round(100 * attr(pca, "percentVar"))
  
  #pca$label <- substring(str_split_fixed(pca$name, "_", 4)[,2], 1,1)
  pca$label <- str_split_fixed(pca$name, "_", 4)[,2]
  #pca$sex <- str_split_fixed(pca$bear_sex, "_", 2)[,2]
  
  # Color = season, Shape = bear
  ggplot(pca, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = season, shape = sex)) +
    geom_point(size = 4) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Active" = "indianred", "Fall" = "orange", "Hibernation" = "steelblue", 
                                  "Spring" = "mediumseagreen", "SpringT2" = "plum")) +
    #geom_text_repel(aes(label = label), size = 3, max.overlaps = 20) +
    xlab(paste0("PC1: ", percentVar[1], "% variance")) +
    ylab(paste0("PC2: ", percentVar[2], "% variance")) +
    #ggtitle(paste0("Non-Depleted: PCA of Top 2,000 Genes")) +
    theme_light(base_size = 18) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),    
          panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  
  ggsave("blood_pca.pdf", dpi = 600, width = 8, height = 5)

}

pca_nd <- plot_PCA(dds.nondepleted) 
pca_nd

```


pca trajectory
```{r}
plot_PCA_trajectory <- function(dds) {
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggrepel)
  library(dplyr)
  
  # Variance stabilizing transformation
  vsd <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds, blind = TRUE)
  mat <- assay(vsd)

  # Remove individual effect but keep seasonal effect
  mm <- model.matrix(~season, colData(vsd))
  mat <- limma::removeBatchEffect(mat, batch = vsd$bear, design = mm)
  assay(vsd) <- mat
  
  # PCA
  pca <- plotPCA(vsd, intgroup = c("season", "bear", "sex"), returnData = TRUE, ntop = 2000)
  percentVar <- round(100 * attr(pca, "percentVar"))
  
  pca$bear_label <- substring(pca$bear,1, 1)
  
  # Extract bear ID for labeling
  pca$label <- str_split_fixed(pca$name, "_", 4)[,2]
  
  # Define seasonal order explicitly (important for trajectory)
  pca$season <- factor(pca$season, 
                       levels = c("Active", "Fall", "Hibernation", "Spring", "SpringT2"))
  
  # Only keep bears with ≥ 2 samples so geom_path has something to connect
  pca_for_path <- pca %>% group_by(bear_label) %>% filter(n() > 1)
  
  ggplot(pca, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = season, shape = sex)) +
    geom_path(
      data = pca_for_path,
      aes(group = bear_label),
      linewidth = 1,
      alpha = 0.6,
      arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "cm"))
    ) +
    geom_point(size = 4) +
    #geom_text_repel(aes(label = label), size = 3, max.overlaps = 20) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
      "Active" = "indianred",
      "Fall" = "orange",
      "Hibernation" = "steelblue",
      "Spring" = "mediumseagreen",
      "SpringT2" = "plum"
    )) +
    xlab(paste0("PC1: ", percentVar[1], "% variance")) +
    ylab(paste0("PC2: ", percentVar[2], "% variance")) +
    facet_wrap(~ bear_label, ncol = 3) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
    ggtitle("PCA Trajectories Across Seasons (Top 2,000 Genes)")
  ggsave("Supp_Figure5_final.pdf",width = 8, height = 5, dpi=600)
}

# Run for depleted and non-depleted
plot_PCA_trajectory(dds.nondepleted)

#plot_PCA_trajectory(dds.depleted)
```

# Extract results
```{r}
get_all_season_contrasts <- function(dds, seasons) {
  library(IHW)
  library(clusterProfiler)
  library(tidyverse)
  res_list <- list()
  sig_res_list <- list()
  resLFC_list <- list()
  #resLFC <- lfcShrink(dds, coef="condition_treated_vs_untreated", type="apeglm")

  for (i in seasons) {
    for (j in seasons) {
      if (i != j) {  # skip self-comparisons
        contrast_name <- paste0(i, "_vs_", j)
        print(paste0("Contrasting ", i, " vs ", j))
        res <- results(dds, contrast = c("season", i, j),filterFun = ihw) # res_list[[contrast_name]]
        
        # symbol_to_entrez <- bitr(rownames(res), fromType = "SYMBOL", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb = bear_annot)
        # res$ENTREZID <- symbol_to_entrez$ENTREZID
      
        
        resOrdered <- res[order(res$pvalue), ]
        
        resOrdered_sig <- as.data.frame(resOrdered) %>% filter(padj < 0.05)
        
        
        res_list[[contrast_name]] <- as.data.frame(res)
        sig_res_list[[contrast_name]] <- resOrdered_sig
      }
    }
  }
  print("Done!")
  
  return(list(res = res_list, sig_res = sig_res_list))
}

# Usage
seasons <- c("Active", "Fall","Hibernation","Spring","SpringT2")

#depleted_res <- get_all_season_contrasts(dds.depleted, seasons)

full_res <- get_all_season_contrasts(dds.nondepleted, seasons)
nondepleted_res <- full_res$res
nondepleted_resOrdered_sig <- full_res$sig_res
```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)

# Define the comparisons you want to keep (only one direction)
keep_comparisons <- c(
  "Active_vs_Fall",
  "Active_vs_Hibernation",
  "Active_vs_Spring",
  "Active_vs_SpringT2",
  "Fall_vs_Hibernation",
  "Fall_vs_Spring",
  "Fall_vs_SpringT2",
  "Hibernation_vs_Spring",
  "Hibernation_vs_SpringT2",
  "Spring_vs_SpringT2"
)

# Extract and combine results
supp_table <- map_dfr(
  keep_comparisons,
  function(comp) {
    df <- full_res$res[[comp]]
    df$gene <- rownames(df)
    df$comparison <- comp
    df
  }
)

# Reorder columns so comparison + gene come first
supp_table <- supp_table %>%
  select(comparison, gene, everything())

# Save
write.csv(supp_table, "pairwise_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```



```{r}
# Extract all rownames from all significant results
all_sig_genes <- unlist(
  lapply(nondepleted_resOrdered_sig, function(df) rownames(df))
)

# Unique genes across all contrasts
unique_sig_genes <- unique(all_sig_genes)

# Count
length(unique_sig_genes)
table(all_sig_genes)
```


Alluvial: plotting the sequential comparisons
```{r}
sequential_df <- count_df %>%
  filter(str_detect(season,
                    "Active_vs_Fall|Fall_vs_Hibernation|Hibernation_vs_Spring|Spring_vs_SpringT2")) %>% # |SpringT2_vs_Active
  filter(!str_detect(season, "Hibernation_vs_SpringT2"))  # exclude this comparison

ggplot(sequential_df, aes(x = season, y = n, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Comparison", y = "Number of Genes",
       title = "DEG Counts per Comparison") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Up" = "indianred3",
                               "Down" = "steelblue",
                               "Not Sig" = "grey70")) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


```
